notes: Show-level bench for the curation engine, per docs/brief/03_CURATION_SPEC.md. Carried-over shows (already referenced in data/session.json) were not re-verified via iTunes -- only their existing apple_collection_id was reused, plus feed_url already verified in docs/research/fusion-candidates.md and docs/research/other-slot-candidates.md by prior research passes in this project. All newly added shows were verified fresh via https://itunes.apple.com/search and https://itunes.apple.com/lookup; no collection id or feed url in this file was invented. cadence_hint is inferred from each show's most recent indexed release date (via iTunes lookup) relative to built_at, corroborated by trackCount and, where noted, public knowledge of the show's known publishing rhythm -- treat it as an approximation, not a guarantee.

shows.title: Lex Fridman Podcast
shows.editorial_note: Marathon technical interviews with the actual scientists and founders building fusion reactors, AI, and everything adjacent.

shows.title: Titans of Nuclear
shows.editorial_note: The widest bench in fusion/fission podcasting -- a new reactor architecture or company nearly every episode.

shows.title: omega tau
shows.editorial_note: The most technically dense engineering-tour podcast in English -- stellarators, magnets, superconductivity, no hand-holding.

shows.title: CleanTechies Podcast
shows.editorial_note: Shorter founder interviews across the clean-energy startup landscape, fusion included.

shows.title: Catalyst with Shayle Kann
shows.editorial_note: Energy-transition analyst digs into the engineering and market mechanics of new power technologies.

shows.title: Lab to Market Leadership
shows.editorial_note: How deep-tech founders take a lab result to a company -- fusion and hard-tech leadership stories.

shows.title: CBC Ideas
shows.editorial_note: CBC's long-running ideas documentary strand -- occasionally lands on fusion, energy, and technology with a narrative, current-affairs voice.

shows.title: Stuff You Should Know
shows.editorial_note: Reliable general-audience explainer show -- easy entry points into metallurgy, materials, and technology history topics.

shows.title: Materialism: A Materials Science Podcast
shows.editorial_note: The dedicated materials-science show -- history, commercialization, and characterization of the stuff things are made of.

shows.title: Inside Chips (Semiconductor Engineering)
shows.editorial_note: B2B semiconductor-fab panel talk -- precision process control that rhymes with reactor-grade manufacturing.

shows.title: MTDCNC Podcast
shows.editorial_note: UK precision-machining trade show -- interviews with the people who design, build, and operate CNC and aerospace-grade equipment.

shows.title: Being an Engineer
shows.editorial_note: Product-engineering war stories, CAD to tooling to production, on everything from medical devices to a coffee tamper.

shows.title: Fall of Civilizations Podcast
shows.editorial_note: The benchmark long-form documentary history show -- multi-hour rise-and-fall sagas with real engineering and military detail.

shows.title: The Rest Is History
shows.editorial_note: Sandbrook and Holland's conversational history juggernaut -- broad chronological coverage, strong narrative pacing.

shows.title: Conan O'Brien Needs a Friend
shows.editorial_note: Conan's real-friendship-with-guests premise -- consistently the warmest, easiest hang in the catalog.

shows.title: SmartLess
shows.editorial_note: Bateman, Hayes, and Arnett's surprise-guest hang -- low pressure, high chemistry.

shows.title: My Brother, My Brother and Me
shows.editorial_note: Three real brothers answering advice questions with pure absurdism -- zero stakes, guaranteed laughs.

shows.title: Making Chips Podcast for Manufacturing Leaders
shows.editorial_note: Machine-shop leadership talk -- how precision shops actually quote, hire, and scale, from people who run them.

shows.title: Business of Machining
shows.editorial_note: Two shop owners talking CNC economics, tooling choices, and the unglamorous daily grind of running a machine shop.

shows.title: Within Tolerance
shows.editorial_note: Metrology and quality-control nerdery -- measurement uncertainty and inspection for people who care about tenths of a thousandth.

shows.title: MRS Bulletin Materials News Podcast
shows.editorial_note: Materials Research Society's news show -- quick, credentialed hits on new-materials breakthroughs across biomaterials, quantum, and energy.

shows.title: It's a Material World: Materials Science Podcast
shows.editorial_note: Panel conversations on how engineered materials quietly reshape everyday industries -- broad rather than deep.

shows.title: Dan Carlin's Hardcore History
shows.editorial_note: The gold standard for narrative military history -- multi-hour, meticulously researched sagas, ancient through modern.

shows.title: The Ancients
shows.editorial_note: History Hit's flagship ancient-world show -- Tristan Hughes runs down a civilization, battle, or discovery with an academic guest.

shows.title: Ancient History Fangirl
shows.editorial_note: Ancient history and mythology told with storyteller's pacing rather than a lecture's.

shows.title: The History of Ancient Greece
shows.editorial_note: Chronological deep dive through Greek military and political history, Bronze Age through Roman conquest.

shows.title: Engines of Our Ingenuity
shows.editorial_note: Bite-sized (5-minute) stories of inventions and the people behind them -- good filler between longer picks.

shows.title: The Engineering History Podcast
shows.editorial_note: Where engineering and history collide -- from the Golden Gate Bridge to the Boston Molasses Flood.

shows.title: Acquired
shows.editorial_note: 3-5 hour definitive company histories with real primary research -- deep-tech business strategy done properly.

shows.title: Founders
shows.editorial_note: David Senra reads founder biographies and pulls out the operating lessons -- obsessive, opinionated, prolific.

shows.title: This Week in Startups
shows.editorial_note: Jason Calacanis's long-running startup news and interview show -- a pulse check on where venture money is flowing.

shows.title: TechSurge: Deep Tech Podcast
shows.editorial_note: VC-hosted show on frontier technology -- AI, robotics, and the energy/hard-science startups behind them.

shows.title: The Twenty Minute VC (20VC)
shows.editorial_note: Harry Stebbings' venture-capital interview marathon -- useful for the funding and market-dynamics side of deep tech.

shows.title: Luthier on Luthier with Michael Bashkin
shows.editorial_note: One guitar builder interviewing another -- bracing, wood selection, and the small decisions that make an instrument sing.

shows.title: The Fretboard Journal Guitar Podcast
shows.editorial_note: Long-running guitar-culture interviews that regularly drift into build technique and lutherie history.

shows.title: The Violin Chronicles Podcast
shows.editorial_note: The craft and history of violin making, from historic Cremonese workshops to modern luthiers.

shows.title: Piano Tech Radio Hour
shows.editorial_note: Piano technicians and rebuilders talk tuning, regulation, and instrument restoration in real technical depth.

shows.title: GardenFork Radio - Eclectic DIY
shows.editorial_note: Eclectic, low-key DIY -- home fixes, workshop tips, cooking, and gardening in short practical bursts.

shows.title: Around the House with Eric G
shows.editorial_note: Call-in home-improvement radio energy -- practical answers to real renovation and repair questions.

shows.title: Wood Talk | Woodworking
shows.editorial_note: Three working woodworkers riffing on tools, technique, and shop mistakes -- funnier than a how-to show has any right to be.

shows.title: Shop Talk Live - Fine Woodworking
shows.editorial_note: Fine Woodworking magazine's staff panel answering listener questions with real technique depth.

shows.title: Good Hang with Amy Poehler
shows.editorial_note: Amy Poehler catching up with famous friends -- warm, unhurried, zero homework required.

shows.title: Fly on the Wall with Dana Carvey and David Spade
shows.editorial_note: Two old SNL/showbiz friends swapping stories and characters -- easy, silly, industry-insider hang.

shows.title: Bad Friends
shows.editorial_note: Andrew Santino and Bobby Lee roasting each other -- spikier energy than the other comfort picks for days that need more jolt.

shows.title: Gastropod
shows.editorial_note: Two science journalists dissect food chemistry and history -- a stretch pick for engineering minds.

shows.title: The BBQ Central Show
shows.editorial_note: Long-running BBQ radio show with pitmasters and technique talk -- the answer to a barbecue request.

shows.title: Black Box Down
shows.editorial_note: Two hosts dissect major aviation disasters and the engineering fixes that followed each one.

shows.title: Flight Safety Detectives
shows.editorial_note: Former NTSB investigators break down real crash investigations and the safety systems behind them.

shows.title: This Podcast Will Kill You
shows.editorial_note: Two epidemiologists cover one disease a week -- biology, history, and how outbreaks actually spread.

shows.title: Two Scientists Walk Into a Bar
shows.editorial_note: Genentech researchers translate frontier biology -- AI drug discovery, gene therapy, cancer -- into plain talk.

shows.title: Lingthusiasm - A podcast that's enthusiastic about linguistics
shows.editorial_note: Two linguists dig into how language actually works, from grammar structure to internet slang.

shows.title: The Allusionist
shows.editorial_note: Helen Zaltzman on the strange histories tangled up inside ordinary words and phrases.

shows.title: Science for Sport Podcast
shows.editorial_note: Applied sports-science interviews on the physiology and engineering behind elite athletic performance.

shows.title: Just Fly Performance Podcast
shows.editorial_note: Long-form coaching interviews on the biomechanics of speed, power, and athletic movement.

shows.title: The Outlaw Ocean
shows.editorial_note: Investigative reporting from the world's most lawless waters -- crime, labor, and geopolitics at sea.

shows.title: The Sound Aquatic
shows.editorial_note: Short, well-produced series on how marine animals use sound to navigate and communicate.

shows.title: Song Exploder
shows.editorial_note: Musicians take one song apart, track by track, to show exactly how it was built.

shows.title: Switched on Pop
shows.editorial_note: Musicologists analyze pop hits for the theory and production choices that make them work.

shows.title: My Favorite Theorem
shows.editorial_note: Mathematicians pick a favorite theorem and explain why it delights them, no lecture required.

shows.title: 99% Invisible
shows.editorial_note: Roman Mars's design-and-infrastructure show -- the hidden systems and decisions behind the built world.

shows.title: The World's Best Construction Podcast
shows.editorial_note: The B1M's hosts riff on megaprojects, skyscrapers, and infrastructure news from around the world.

shows.title: Spit and Twitches: The Animal Cognition Podcast
shows.editorial_note: Researchers discuss their own animal-cognition studies -- parrots, primates, spiders, and beyond.

shows.title: Ologies with Alie Ward
shows.editorial_note: Alie Ward interviews an expert '-ologist' each week on one absurdly specific scientific niche.

shows.title: SpyCast
shows.editorial_note: International Spy Museum's official show -- tradecraft, Cold War history, and modern espionage tech.

shows.title: Main Engine Cut Off
shows.editorial_note: Anthony Colangelo's near-daily rundown of rocket-industry news -- sharp technical reads on NASA, SpaceX, and the rest of the launch business.

shows.title: Off-Nominal
shows.editorial_note: Two space-industry insiders riff on spaceflight news with technical fluency and genuine friendship.

shows.title: Planetary Radio: Space Exploration, Astronomy and Science
shows.editorial_note: The Planetary Society's flagship show -- working scientists and engineers on missions across the solar system.

shows.title: Advent of Computing
shows.editorial_note: A historian traces forgotten programming languages, machines, and the people who built early computing culture.

shows.title: CoRecursive: Coding Stories
shows.editorial_note: Software engineers tell the real stories behind the code -- bugs, breakthroughs, and the humans who shipped them.

shows.title: Software Engineering Daily
shows.editorial_note: Daily interviews with practicing engineers on the infrastructure, languages, and systems running modern software.

shows.title: Planet Money
shows.editorial_note: NPR's economics desk turns markets, policy, and money into stories with real stakes.

shows.title: Odd Lots
shows.editorial_note: Bloomberg's Joe Weisenthal and Tracy Alloway go deep on markets with the people who actually trade them.

shows.title: Freakonomics Radio
shows.editorial_note: Stephen Dubner applies economic reasoning to questions far outside traditional economics.

shows.title: Hidden Brain
shows.editorial_note: Shankar Vedantam on the unconscious patterns that quietly steer human behavior.

shows.title: You Are Not So Smart
shows.editorial_note: David McRaney on cognitive bias, self-delusion, and how people actually change their minds.

shows.title: Choiceology with Katy Milkman
shows.editorial_note: Behavioral economist Katy Milkman uses real stories to unpack why people choose the way they do.

shows.title: PlanetGeo: The Geology Podcast
shows.editorial_note: Two geologists dig into granite, geochronology, and the debates that shaped modern earth science.

shows.title: Geology Bites
shows.editorial_note: Working geologists explain their own published research, from moon magnetism to ancient plate tectonics.

shows.title: Don't Panic Geocast
shows.editorial_note: A weekly geoscience news roundup with a running paper-of-the-week segment for working researchers.

shows.title: Well There's Your Problem
shows.editorial_note: Three hosts dissect engineering disasters and the organizational failures that caused them, at length.

shows.title: Causality
shows.editorial_note: Documentary-style breakdowns of engineering disasters -- what went wrong, and how it could have been stopped.

shows.title: Brady Heywood Podcast
shows.editorial_note: A forensic engineer investigates real structural failures, from mine collapses to bridge design errors.

shows.title: F1 Beyond The Grid
shows.editorial_note: Former F1 broadcaster Tom Clarkson interviews team principals and engineers shaping Formula 1 strategy.

shows.title: Tuned In
shows.editorial_note: High Performance Academy's motorsport engineers talk tuning, fabrication, and the physics of going fast.

shows.title: The Race F1 Podcast
shows.editorial_note: A former F1 technical director breaks down car design, regulations, and race-weekend engineering calls.

shows.title: Volts
shows.editorial_note: David Roberts interviews the engineers and regulators rebuilding the electric grid around renewables.

shows.title: Energy Gang
shows.editorial_note: Wood Mackenzie analysts and guests debate the politics and economics of the energy transition.

shows.title: Watt It Takes
shows.editorial_note: Founder interviews on the startups electrifying grids, transit, and industrial power.

shows.title: Philosophize This!
shows.editorial_note: Stephen West walks through the history of philosophy one thinker and argument at a time.

shows.title: The Partially Examined Life Philosophy Podcast
shows.editorial_note: Four non-professional philosophers work through primary texts in real, unpolished detail.

shows.title: Philosophy Bites
shows.editorial_note: Short, sharp interviews with working philosophers on one focused question each episode.

shows.title: Camera Shake Photography Podcast
shows.editorial_note: Working photographers talk technique, business, and the craft decisions behind their best work.

shows.title: Go Creative Show
shows.editorial_note: Cinematographers and directors break down how specific films and shows actually got shot.

shows.title: The Cinematography Podcast
shows.editorial_note: Working cinematographers explain the lighting, color, and lens choices behind current film and TV.

shows.title: Radiolab
shows.editorial_note: WNYC's original science-storytelling show, turning curiosity and hard evidence into sound-rich narrative journeys.

shows.title: Science Vs
shows.editorial_note: Wendy Zukerman puts popular claims on trial against the actual scientific evidence.

shows.title: Unexplainable
shows.editorial_note: Vox's science show for the stuff researchers still can't fully explain.

shows.title: How I Built This with Guy Raz
shows.editorial_note: Guy Raz asks founders how they actually built their companies, mistakes included.

shows.title: Business Wars
shows.editorial_note: Head-to-head corporate rivalry stories -- real strategy, real stakes, real winners and losers.

shows.title: Masters of Scale
shows.editorial_note: Reid Hoffman and founders unpack how companies actually scale, one hard lesson at a time.

shows.title: WW2 Pod: We Have Ways of Making You Talk
shows.editorial_note: James Holland and Al Murray's meticulous, funny rundown of World War II campaigns.

shows.title: The Unauthorized History of the Pacific War
shows.editorial_note: A WWII Museum historian and a submarine commodore reconstruct the Pacific War's naval battles.

shows.title: The History of WWII Podcast
shows.editorial_note: Ray Harris Jr's methodical, near-daily march through World War II, battle by battle.

shows.title: The War on Cars
shows.editorial_note: Sarah Goodyear, Doug Gordon, and Aaron Naparstek on the fight between cars and cities.

shows.title: The Strong Towns Podcast
shows.editorial_note: Chuck Marohn's movement rethinking how American towns actually build and pay for infrastructure.

shows.title: Talking Headways: A Streetsblog Podcast
shows.editorial_note: Streetsblog's transit-nerd interview show -- policy, funding, and the politics of getting around.

shows.title: Chemistry World Podcast
shows.editorial_note: The Royal Society of Chemistry's weekly tour of chemistry news and the periodic table.

shows.title: Distillations | Science History Institute
shows.editorial_note: Science History Institute's documentary strand on chemistry's stranger, more human stories.

shows.title: Tiny Matters
shows.editorial_note: ACS-affiliated hosts unpack molecules and microbes with a big, surprising societal reach.

shows.title: the Sharp End Podcast
shows.editorial_note: Real climbers and rescuers narrate the accidents behind the American Alpine Club's annual report.

shows.title: The Enormocast: the climbing podcast
shows.editorial_note: Chris Kalous's long-form conversations with the most interesting people in climbing.

shows.title: Alpinist
shows.editorial_note: Alpinist Magazine's oral-history interviews -- dramatic ascents and the community's sharpest debates.

shows.title: Designer Notes
shows.editorial_note: Soren Johnson interviews veteran game designers about the decisions behind their defining games.

shows.title: The Game Design Round Table
shows.editorial_note: Two working designers cover thematic design, mechanics, and the business behind games.

shows.title: The Clockwork Game Design Podcast
shows.editorial_note: Keith Burgun's opinionated, technical take on strategy games and what makes them work.

shows.title: Huberman Lab
shows.editorial_note: Stanford neuroscientist Andrew Huberman translates lab research into usable protocols for body and mind.

shows.title: The Matt Walker Podcast
shows.editorial_note: UC Berkeley sleep scientist Matt Walker on the neuroscience of rest and its consequences.

shows.title: The Peter Attia Drive
shows.editorial_note: Physician Peter Attia on longevity science -- training, nutrition, and disease risk in real depth.

shows.title: Strict Scrutiny
shows.editorial_note: Three law professors dissect Supreme Court cases with real expertise and sharp opinions.

shows.title: Amicus With Dahlia Lithwick | Law, justice, and the courts
shows.editorial_note: Dahlia Lithwick's long-running Slate show on the Supreme Court and how courts actually work.
A skeptical, sharply argued rundown of Supreme Court rulings from Prologue Projects.

shows.title: Craft Beer & Brewing Magazine Podcast
shows.editorial_note: Trade-magazine interviews with brewers on recipes, technique, and the business of beer.

shows.title: Experimental Brewing
shows.editorial_note: Homebrewing authors Denny Conn and Drew Beechum test ingredients and technique on air.

shows.title: FermUp - The Fermented Food Podcast
shows.editorial_note: Weekly conversations on the art, science, and history of fermenting food at home.
